title: World History
description: Question batch for the Medieval History domain.

questions.question: Which empire preserved the eastern Roman legacy?
questions.explanation: The Byzantine Empire continued the eastern Roman state.

questions.question: What was the capital of the Byzantine Empire?
questions.explanation: Constantinople was the Byzantine capital.

questions.question: Which system tied land to service in medieval Europe?
questions.explanation: Feudalism linked landholding and service.

questions.question: Who were the heavily armored horsemen of medieval Europe?
questions.explanation: Knights were mounted warriors in feudal society.

questions.question: Which document limited the English king in 1215?
questions.explanation: The Magna Carta limited royal power.

questions.question: Which religion spread from Arabia in the 7th century?
questions.explanation: Islam began in Arabia in the 7th century.

questions.question: Which city became a major center of the Abbasid Caliphate?
questions.explanation: Baghdad was a leading Abbasid city.

questions.question: What were the Crusades?
answers: Religious wars over the Holy Land
questions.explanation: The Crusades were military campaigns focused on the Holy Land.

questions.question: Which epidemic devastated Europe in the 14th century?
questions.explanation: The Black Death killed millions in Europe.

questions.question: Which conqueror founded the Mongol Empire?
questions.explanation: Genghis Khan founded the Mongol Empire.

questions.question: Which ruler was crowned emperor in 800 CE?
questions.explanation: Charlemagne was crowned emperor in 800.

questions.question: Which battle in 1066 changed England's ruling class?
questions.explanation: The Norman victory at Hastings reshaped England.

questions.question: Who led the Norman conquest of England?
questions.explanation: William of Normandy conquered England in 1066.

questions.question: What was a medieval manor?
questions.explanation: Manors were local agricultural estates in feudal life.

questions.question: Which Islamic scholar city was famed for learning in Spain?
questions.explanation: Cordoba was a major intellectual center in Al-Andalus.

questions.question: Which empire connected East and West across Eurasia under Mongol rule?
questions.explanation: The Mongol Empire linked much of Eurasia.

questions.question: Which West African ruler is famous for a rich pilgrimage to Mecca?
questions.explanation: Mansa Musa is famous for his wealth and pilgrimage.

questions.question: Which Japanese warrior class dominated medieval Japan?
questions.explanation: Samurai were the warrior elite of medieval Japan.

questions.question: Who ruled Japan in the name of the emperor during feudal times?
questions.explanation: The shogun held military rule in feudal Japan.

questions.question: What was the main role of medieval guilds?
questions.explanation: Guilds organized craft standards and training.

questions.question: Why was Constantinople important?
answers: It linked Europe and Asia trade
questions.explanation: Its location made it a major trade and political center.

questions.question: What weakened feudalism in late medieval Europe?
questions.explanation: Plague, war, and economic change weakened feudal bonds.

questions.question: Why were monasteries important in Europe?
questions.explanation: Monasteries copied texts and preserved scholarship.

questions.question: Which battle stopped a Muslim advance into Frankish lands in 732?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Tours was fought in 732.

questions.question: What was one result of the Crusades?
answers: More exchange between Europe and the East
questions.explanation: The Crusades increased contact, trade, and cultural exchange.

questions.question: Why did Mongol rule aid trade?
questions.explanation: Mongol control often made long-distance trade safer.

questions.question: Which institution dominated learning in medieval Europe?
questions.explanation: The Church shaped education and intellectual life.

questions.question: What was scholasticism?
answers: A method of learning using reason and faith
questions.explanation: Scholasticism tried to reconcile faith and logic.

questions.question: Why was the Black Death historically important?
answers: It changed Europe's population and economy
questions.explanation: Mass death changed labor, society, and power structures.

questions.question: Which empire fell to the Ottomans in 1453?
questions.explanation: Constantinople fell in 1453, ending the Byzantine Empire.
